title: Wireless Redstone: RedstoneLink-makomi
description: A fun and useful wireless redstone mod
body:
[![License: LGPL v3](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-LGPL_v3-blue.svg?logo=gnu)](LICENSE) [![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-release/no--mixin-181717?logo=github&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/makomi-1/redstonelink-makomi/tree/release/no-mixin)

# What it can do?

* Connect the switches on the wall neatly and beautifully to the lights on the ceiling.
* Control multiple remote Redstone machines centrally and name the switches and receive nodes.
* Record and view the Redstone signal curves.
* Visualize the connection diagram of the nodes to be edited.
* Flexibly control the validity of the connections .
* Remotely control a certain Redstone device...  


# Features

* **Stronger wireless pistons: capable of pushing and pulling boxes, furnaces, funnels, and more.**
  
![stronger]()

* **Wirelessly control the original items with the original items themselves.They support all Mod node functions.**

![wireless products](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/7303c7d3ac540adcddd26873fd62d13e8b3db51b.png)

*  **Hidden, no collision**

![hide](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/hide.gif)

* **Directional editing of output and input**

![direct](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/direct.gif)


*   Transmission with `no distance limit`, support for `cross-dimension` operation, and on-demand `force-loading`
*   supports `zero delay` and 0/1/2 `batch packaging` delay windows, and can stably `transmit 0-15 strength signals (sync)`.
*   the `Quick Link Tool`, pairing GUI, web visualization editor, and incremental/overwrite edits all connected in one workflow.
*   the status panel can `track` and `plot I/O curves`, and with K-key debugging you can locate issues quickly.
*   ID and channel capacity up to `2^63-1`, supports `custom node aliases`, channels, and automatically cleans up invalid IDs.
*   multiple triggerSource/core combinations, plus node sets, channels, strength filtering, and `dynamic control`.

# Getting Started

[Item Guide](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/item_guide.html)/[Recipes](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/recipe_sheet.html)  
Nodes are divided into three categories: `triggers`, `connection cores`, and `repeaters`:
The usage method is: 

* **Establishing Connections**: Squat down and right-click to open the pairing UI, or use the `Quick Connection Tool` to configure the serial number or channel.

![how to link](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/link.gif)

* **Activating Transmission**: Activate the trigger through `Redstone Signal Input` to `transmit Redstone signals` to the connected connection core, or use a `repeater` for relaying.

![linker](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/linker.gif)

* **Filtering**: Configure the filter and place it so that its `range of effect` covers the corresponding node, and then activate the filter.

![filter](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/filter.gif)

* **Forced Loading**: Configure the block activator, place it and activate it.

![force load](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/cross_chunk.gif)

* **Recording Signals**: Subscribe to the corresponding node on the status panel, and configure and start the recording.

![record](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/record.gif)

* **Assisted Debugging**: Wear the `Smart Glasses` and use the `K` key to interact with the empty hand interface nodes, thereby switching between displaying node information and establishing connections.

![smart glasses](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/smart_glasses.gif)

* **Recycle and store the prepared items**

![direct](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/collect.gif)
 
* **Visual Editing**: Use the `Graph Visualization Editor`

![Visual Editing](https://makomi-1.github.io/redstonelink-makomi/graph.gif)

# Other Documentation

*   [Mechanism Design](https://github.com/makomi-1/redstonelink-makomi/blob/release/no-mixin/docs/mechanism_design_en.md)
*   [Usage Guide](https://github.com/makomi-1/redstonelink-makomi/blob/release/no-mixin/docs/usage_guide_en.md)
*   Conflict List (to be added)

# Other Features

*   Full-lifecycle signal synchronization: placing, mining, and breaking nodes and filters automatically synchronizes signals (sync)
*   Rich node types: block, plate-like, and transparent receiver nodes, plus triggerSource nodes including buttons, levers, remotes, and different signal types
*   Rich and detailed configuration options, along with commands, permissions, and rate-limiting systems
*   Node items support stacking and quick operations
*   The Quick Link Tool supports GUI configuration, as well as incremental or overwrite edits, and can be applied to nodes and filters …

# Future Plans

*   ☑️Add Send/receive filters
*   ☑️Add recording and playback support to the status panel
*   ☑️Support custom node aliases
*   ☑️Expand channel matching
*   ☑️Improve the UI
*   ❌Migrate to version 26.1+
*   ☑️Add a visual network analyzer
*   ☑️Add a repeater

# Disclaimer and Report

*   This project uses `AI`
*   Currently supports `fabric-0.18.4` and higher.
*   [Conflict Report](https://www.wjx.top/vm/wTsJKow.aspx)
*   [Bug Report](https://www.wjx.top/vm/PpvzYjl.aspx)
*   [Gameplay Feedbak](https://www.wjx.top/vm/OrxsfZA.aspx)
